item.title: Call for Session Proposals for the African Union Innovation Festival (AU InnoFest’26): Help Shape Africa’s Digital Future
item.dc:creator: Dora Kumi
item.description:
The African Union Commission (AUC) has officially opened the Call for Session Proposals for the African Union Innovation Festival 2026 (AU InnoFest’26), inviting innovators, youth organizations, technology experts, entrepreneurs, policymakers, academia, development partners, and ecosystem builders to contribute to one of Africa’s premier innovation and digital transformation events. Scheduled to take place from 29–30 September […]

The post Call for Session Proposals for the African Union Innovation Festival (AU InnoFest’26): Help Shape Africa’s Digital Future appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The African Union Commission (AUC) has officially opened the Call for Session Proposals for the African Union Innovation Festival 2026 (AU InnoFest’26), inviting innovators, youth organizations, technology experts, entrepreneurs, policymakers, academia, development partners, and ecosystem builders to contribute to one of Africa’s premier innovation and digital transformation events. Scheduled to take place from 29–30 September 2026 at the African Union Commission Headquarters in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, AU InnoFest’26 will convene innovators, policymakers, business leaders, researchers, and young entrepreneurs from across Africa to explore solutions that will shape the continent’s digital future.

Under the theme “Vibrant Youth Ecosystems for AI and Digital Sovereignty,” the festival aims to strengthen youth-led innovation ecosystems while advancing Africa’s ambitions in Artificial Intelligence (AI), digital transformation, technological innovation, and digital sovereignty.

If you have a compelling session idea that can inspire meaningful dialogue, foster collaboration, or promote innovative solutions, this is your opportunity to help shape the official conference agenda.

About AU InnoFest’26

The African Union Innovation Festival (AU InnoFest) is the African Union’s flagship platform dedicated to youth innovation, entrepreneurship, emerging technologies, and digital transformation across Africa.

The event serves as a meeting point for:

  • Young innovators
  • Technology entrepreneurs
  • Policymakers
  • Researchers
  • Development organizations
  • Government institutions
  • Civil society
  • Private sector leaders
  • Innovation hubs
  • Academia
  • International partners

Through interactive sessions, networking opportunities, policy discussions, exhibitions, and collaborative engagements, AU InnoFest seeks to accelerate Africa’s digital transformation agenda while empowering young Africans to become leaders of technological innovation.

Theme of AU InnoFest’26

The 2026 edition will be held under the theme:

“Vibrant Youth Ecosystems for AI and Digital Sovereignty.”

The theme reflects Africa’s growing commitment to:

  • Artificial Intelligence innovation
  • Digital transformation
  • Youth empowerment
  • Home-grown technological solutions
  • Digital inclusion
  • Policy development
  • Responsible AI
  • Innovation ecosystems
  • Skills development
  • Cross-sector collaboration

Festival Objective

The primary objective of AU InnoFest’26 is to strengthen vibrant youth-led ecosystems that advance Africa’s AI capabilities and digital sovereignty through:

  • Innovation
  • Policy engagement
  • Strategic partnerships
  • Capacity building
  • Digital skills development
  • Knowledge sharing
  • Collaborative action

The festival recognizes that Africa’s digital future depends on empowering young innovators to create locally relevant technological solutions while influencing policies that promote sustainable digital growth across the continent.

Why Submit a Session Proposal?

Selected session organizers will have the opportunity to:

  • Contribute to the official AU InnoFest’26 programme.
  • Share innovative ideas with a continental audience.
  • Showcase successful projects and initiatives.
  • Lead discussions on emerging digital trends.
  • Connect with policymakers, investors, innovators, and development partners.
  • Influence conversations on Africa’s AI and digital transformation agenda.
  • Expand professional networks across Africa.
  • Increase visibility for their organization and initiatives.

Who Should Submit a Proposal?

The African Union welcomes session proposals from a broad range of stakeholders involved in innovation and digital development.

Potential applicants include:

  • Youth organizations
  • Innovation hubs
  • Startup founders
  • Universities
  • Research institutions
  • Technology companies
  • Civil society organizations
  • Government agencies
  • Development partners
  • Entrepreneurs
  • Artificial Intelligence experts
  • Digital transformation specialists
  • Policy professionals
  • Community leaders
  • Regional organizations

What Makes a Strong Session Proposal?

Successful proposals should align with the overall objectives of AU InnoFest’26 by encouraging meaningful discussions around:

  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Digital sovereignty
  • Innovation ecosystems
  • Youth entrepreneurship
  • Digital inclusion
  • Technology policy
  • Skills development
  • Emerging technologies
  • Digital governance
  • Cross-sector partnerships
  • Startup ecosystems
  • Future workforce development

Proposals should demonstrate clear relevance to the conference theme while offering practical insights and opportunities for audience engagement.

Important Information Before You Apply

Applicants should carefully prepare all required information before beginning the submission process.

You must have the following details ready:

  • Full names of all proposed speakers
  • Professional titles or designations
  • Email addresses of speakers
  • Moderator information
  • Panelist details

Having this information available before starting the application will help ensure a smooth submission process.

Data Protection and Privacy

The African Union Commission places significant importance on protecting applicants’ personal information.

Information collected through the Call for Session Proposals will be used solely for:

  • Reviewing submissions
  • Evaluating proposals
  • Managing the selection process
  • Administering AU InnoFest’26

Personal information collected may include:

  • Names
  • Contact information
  • Professional affiliations
  • Speaker biographies

Access to this information will be restricted to:

  • Authorized members of the AU InnoFest’26 organizing team
  • Approved programme review partners involved in the evaluation process

If a proposal is selected, certain information may be published in:

  • Official conference programmes
  • Promotional materials
  • Event communications
  • Speaker profiles
  • Conference schedules

The African Union Commission confirms that personal information will not be shared with third parties for commercial purposes.

Applicant Responsibilities

By submitting a proposal, applicants confirm that:

  • They have obtained consent from every proposed speaker.
  • All submitted information is accurate.
  • They agree to the African Union Commission processing the submitted information for the purposes of AU InnoFest’26.

Applicants are encouraged to verify all information before submission to avoid delays during the review process.

Why AU InnoFest Matters

Africa is entering a transformative period where Artificial Intelligence, digital innovation, and technological sovereignty are becoming central to economic development.

AU InnoFest provides an important platform for:

  • Encouraging youth leadership.
  • Promoting African innovation.
  • Strengthening digital ecosystems.
  • Influencing policy development.
  • Supporting entrepreneurship.
  • Building partnerships across sectors.
  • Showcasing African technological solutions.
  • Advancing digital inclusion.

The festival serves as a catalyst for collaboration between governments, innovators, researchers, investors, and young entrepreneurs working toward a digitally empowered Africa.

How to Apply

Interested individuals and organizations should submit their session proposals through the official AU InnoFest’26 application form HERE

Before submitting your proposal, ensure that you:

  1. Develop a session idea that aligns with the theme “Vibrant Youth Ecosystems for AI and Digital Sovereignty.”
  2. Identify and confirm your panelists, speakers, and moderators.
  3. Obtain the consent of all proposed speakers before sharing their personal information.
  4. Prepare the required information, including:
    • Names of speakers
    • Professional titles/designations
    • Email addresses
    • Session description
  5. Complete the official application form with accurate and complete information.
  6. Review your submission carefully before final submission.

DEADLINE: 11th August, 2026

Event Details

Event: African Union Innovation Festival (AU InnoFest’26)

Dates: 29–30 September 2026

Venue: African Union Commission Headquarters

Location: Addis Ababa, Ethiopia

Theme: Vibrant Youth Ecosystems for AI and Digital Sovereignty

Conclusion

The African Union Innovation Festival (AU InnoFest’26) offers innovators, entrepreneurs, policymakers, researchers, youth organizations, and digital ecosystem builders a unique opportunity to shape Africa’s conversations around Artificial Intelligence, digital transformation, and technological sovereignty. By submitting a session proposal, applicants can contribute directly to one of the continent’s most influential innovation platforms while sharing knowledge, fostering collaboration, and inspiring solutions that advance Africa’s digital future. If your organization has expertise, innovative ideas, or successful initiatives that can help strengthen youth-led AI and digital ecosystems, now is the time to prepare a compelling session proposal and become part of AU InnoFest’26.

For more GLOBAL CONFERENCE click HERE

The post Call for Session Proposals for the African Union Innovation Festival (AU InnoFest’26): Help Shape Africa’s Digital Future appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Telecel Ghana National Service Program 2026/2027 for Fresh Graduates in Ghana
item.dc:creator: Dora Kumi
item.description:
Are you a recent graduate preparing to undertake your mandatory National Service in Ghana? The Telecel Ghana National Service Program 2026/2027 presents an exciting opportunity for talented young graduates to gain practical work experience in one of Ghana’s leading telecommunications companies. Designed to bridge the gap between academic learning and professional employment, the Telecel Ghana […]

The post Telecel Ghana National Service Program 2026/2027 for Fresh Graduates in Ghana appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a recent graduate preparing to undertake your mandatory National Service in Ghana? The Telecel Ghana National Service Program 2026/2027 presents an exciting opportunity for talented young graduates to gain practical work experience in one of Ghana’s leading telecommunications companies.

Designed to bridge the gap between academic learning and professional employment, the Telecel Ghana National Service Program offers graduates the opportunity to work on real business projects, develop highly sought-after professional skills, collaborate with experienced industry professionals, and contribute to innovative solutions that impact millions of customers across Ghana.

If you are passionate about technology, business, innovation, customer experience, and personal growth, this National Service opportunity could be the perfect start to your career.

About the Telecel Ghana National Service Program

Telecel Ghana is committed to connecting people and businesses through reliable and innovative telecommunications services. As one of Ghana’s leading telecommunications providers, the company continues to invest in young talent by providing structured learning and career development opportunities. The National Service Program is specifically designed for graduates who are undertaking their mandatory National Service during the 2026–2027 service year.

Participants will work within different departments across the organization, where they will gain hands-on experience while contributing to meaningful business initiatives.

The program combines workplace learning, professional development, teamwork, and exposure to real-world business operations in a dynamic corporate environment.

Why You Should Apply

The Telecel Ghana National Service Program offers more than just a National Service placement.

Successful applicants will have the opportunity to:

  • Gain practical industry experience.
  • Work with one of Ghana’s leading telecommunications companies.
  • Develop technical and professional workplace skills.
  • Collaborate with experienced professionals across multiple departments.
  • Build valuable professional networks.
  • Improve communication and leadership abilities.
  • Participate in impactful business projects.
  • Enhance their employability after National Service.
  • Experience a collaborative and innovative work environment.

Roles and Responsibilities

National Service personnel selected for the program will support the day-to-day operations of their assigned departments while contributing to organizational goals.

Responsibilities include:

1. Support Daily Departmental Operations

Participants will assist with the routine activities and operational functions of their assigned departments to ensure efficient service delivery.

2. Assist with Business Projects

National Service personnel will support ongoing projects by:

  • Conducting research.
  • Collecting and analyzing data.
  • Preparing reports.
  • Coordinating administrative activities.
  • Supporting project implementation.

3. Collaborate Across Teams

Successful candidates will work closely with colleagues from different departments to ensure smooth execution of organizational initiatives.

This collaborative environment allows participants to gain exposure to various aspects of the telecommunications industry.

4. Contribute Innovative Ideas

Telecel Ghana encourages fresh graduates to contribute innovative thinking by:

  • Identifying opportunities for improvement.
  • Suggesting practical solutions.
  • Supporting innovation within their teams.
  • Bringing new perspectives to workplace challenges.

5. Demonstrate Telecel Ghana’s Core Values

Throughout the National Service period, participants are expected to uphold the organization’s core values:

  • Integrity
  • Care
  • Agility
  • Collaboration
  • Innovation

These values form the foundation of Telecel Ghana’s culture and guide everyday interactions within the workplace.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must satisfy the following requirements before applying.

Educational Qualification

Applicants should possess either:

  • A Bachelor’s degree; or
  • A Higher National Diploma (HND)

in any of the following fields:

  • Business Administration
  • Human Resources
  • Marketing
  • Finance
  • Accounting
  • Engineering
  • Computer Science
  • Information Technology (IT)
  • Communications
  • Related disciplines

National Service Eligibility

Applicants must be officially eligible to undertake the 2026–2027 National Service in Ghana.

Professional Skills

Candidates should demonstrate:

  • Excellent written communication skills.
  • Strong verbal communication skills.
  • Microsoft Word proficiency.
  • Microsoft Excel proficiency.
  • Microsoft PowerPoint proficiency.
  • Strong organizational abilities.
  • Analytical thinking.
  • Attention to detail.
  • Professional work ethics.

Personal Attributes

Telecel Ghana is looking for graduates who possess:

  • Leadership potential.
  • Adaptability.
  • Positive attitude.
  • Strong willingness to learn.
  • Integrity.
  • Teamwork skills.
  • Ability to work independently.
  • Commitment to professionalism.

Skills That Will Strengthen Your Application

Although not mandatory beyond the stated requirements, applicants with the following competencies may be more competitive:

  • Data analysis
  • Report writing
  • Research skills
  • Problem-solving
  • Customer service
  • Time management
  • Project coordination
  • Digital literacy
  • Communication skills
  • Microsoft Office proficiency

Working at Telecel Ghana

Telecel Ghana provides an environment that encourages innovation, collaboration, and continuous learning.

National Service personnel will have opportunities to:

  • Learn from experienced professionals.
  • Understand corporate business operations.
  • Develop workplace confidence.
  • Build transferable career skills.
  • Gain exposure to Ghana’s telecommunications industry.
  • Participate in meaningful projects that create business value.

The company seeks to nurture young professionals who can contribute positively while preparing for long-term career success.

Commitment to Diversity and Equal Opportunity

Telecel Ghana is an equal opportunity employer.

The company welcomes applications from qualified individuals without discrimination based on:

  • Race
  • Ethnicity
  • Colour
  • Gender
  • Age
  • Religion
  • Disability
  • Or any other legally protected status.

This commitment ensures an inclusive workplace where talent, performance, and potential are valued above all else.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should submit their application HERE

Before applying, ensure that you:

  1. Confirm your eligibility for the 2026–2027 National Service.
  2. Prepare an updated CV highlighting your academic achievements, leadership experience, technical skills, internships, and extracurricular activities.
  3. Gather any supporting documents that may be required during the recruitment process.
  4. Complete the online application form accurately.
  5. Submit your application through the official recruitment portal before applications close.

DEADLINE: 30th July, 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible to maximize their chances of consideration.

For more information about this opportunity click HERE

Why This Opportunity Matters

For many graduates, National Service serves as the first step into professional employment. Completing your National Service with Telecel Ghana provides valuable exposure to one of Ghana’s most innovative industries while helping you develop technical expertise, leadership skills, workplace professionalism, and business experience.

Whether your career interests lie in business, technology, engineering, finance, communications, marketing, or human resources, this program offers an excellent platform to launch your professional journey.

Conclusion

The Telecel Ghana National Service Program 2026/2027 is an outstanding opportunity for fresh graduates seeking meaningful work experience in Ghana’s telecommunications sector. Through practical assignments, cross-functional collaboration, and mentorship from experienced professionals, participants will develop valuable technical and professional competencies that extend far beyond the National Service year. If you are eligible for the 2026–2027 National Service and are eager to build your career in a dynamic, innovative, and inclusive work environment, this is an opportunity worth pursuing. Prepare a strong application, showcase your academic achievements and leadership experiences, and apply early to begin your professional journey with Telecel Ghana.

For more information on GLOBAL JOBS click HERE

The post Telecel Ghana National Service Program 2026/2027 for Fresh Graduates in Ghana appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Yale Maurice R. Greenberg World Fellows Program 2027: Nominate Outstanding Global Leaders for One of Yale University’s Prestigious Leadership Programmes
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Yale Maurice R. Greenberg World Fellows Program 2027 is inviting nominations for exceptional emerging leaders from around the world who are making a meaningful impact in their fields and have the potential to become influential global changemakers. Hosted by the International Leadership Center at the Yale Jackson School of Global Affairs, the World Fellows […]

The post Yale Maurice R. Greenberg World Fellows Program 2027: Nominate Outstanding Global Leaders for One of Yale University’s Prestigious Leadership Programmes appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Yale Maurice R. Greenberg World Fellows Program 2027 is inviting nominations for exceptional emerging leaders from around the world who are making a meaningful impact in their fields and have the potential to become influential global changemakers. Hosted by the International Leadership Center at the Yale Jackson School of Global Affairs, the World Fellows Program seeks individuals who are challenging conventional thinking, creating innovative solutions, and addressing some of the world’s most pressing issues.

Although the official application period for the 2027 cohort has not yet opened, Yale University is encouraging members of the public to nominate outstanding candidates who they believe would make excellent World Fellows. The nomination process helps identify dynamic leaders from diverse backgrounds and sectors while raising awareness of the programme among potential applicants.

Importantly, nominations are not required to apply for the programme when applications open, nor do they provide any advantage during the selection process. Instead, they serve as a way to ensure that exceptional leaders worldwide become aware of this prestigious opportunity.

About the Yale Maurice R. Greenberg World Fellows Program

The Maurice R. Greenberg World Fellows Program is a flagship international leadership initiative administered by Yale University through its International Leadership Center.

The programme is designed to bring together accomplished professionals from different countries, sectors, and disciplines who have already demonstrated significant impact and are poised to become even more influential leaders.

Rather than focusing solely on academic achievements or professional titles, the programme values individuals who have demonstrated creativity, innovation, leadership, and a commitment to solving global challenges.

Each year, Yale searches for leaders who are:

  • Creating innovative solutions to complex problems.
  • Challenging outdated ideas and approaches.
  • Demonstrating measurable impact in their communities or professions.
  • Growing rapidly in their careers.
  • Committed to expanding their leadership capacity.
  • Motivated to contribute to positive global change.

The programme welcomes individuals from a broad range of professional sectors, recognizing that today’s global challenges require multidisciplinary leadership.

Purpose of the Nomination Process

The nomination process is intended to help Yale identify promising leaders who may benefit from participating in the World Fellows Program.

Rather than relying solely on individuals discovering the programme independently, Yale encourages colleagues, mentors, supervisors, friends, and professional networks to recommend leaders whose work deserves wider recognition.

The nomination initiative aims to:

  • Expand awareness of the World Fellows Program.
  • Identify outstanding emerging leaders globally.
  • Increase diversity among future applicants.
  • Reach talented individuals from different professional backgrounds.
  • Encourage exceptional leaders to apply once applications officially open.

What Kind of Leaders Is Yale Looking For?

According to Yale, ideal World Fellow candidates are individuals who have already begun making a significant difference and continue to demonstrate exceptional promise.

Successful nominees are expected to be people who:

  • Think creatively and challenge conventional approaches.
  • Develop innovative solutions to important problems.
  • Demonstrate measurable impact in their work.
  • Show strong leadership potential.
  • Continue to grow professionally.
  • Aspire to create lasting positive change.

The programme seeks leaders who are not only accomplished but also eager to maximize their future impact.

Sectors Represented by Potential World Fellows

Yale’s search for World Fellows spans numerous professional disciplines.

Potential nominees may come from areas such as:

  • Government
  • Public policy
  • International development
  • Civil society
  • Business
  • Entrepreneurship
  • Technology
  • Journalism
  • Media
  • Education
  • Research
  • Public health
  • Law
  • Arts and culture
  • Environmental sustainability
  • Social innovation
  • Human rights
  • Community development
  • International organizations

The diversity of sectors reflects Yale’s belief that solving global problems requires collaboration across different fields of expertise.

Who Can Submit a Nomination?

One of the programme’s most inclusive features is that anyone can submit a nomination.

Yale specifically notes that:

  • Affiliation with Yale University is not required.
  • Nominations are welcomed from people worldwide.
  • Individuals may nominate more than one candidate.
  • Anyone who knows a suitable leader is encouraged to submit a nomination.

This open approach helps identify talented leaders from every region of the world.

Important Nomination Guidelines

Prospective nominators should keep the following guidelines in mind before submitting a nomination.

1. Self-Nominations Are Not Accepted

Individuals should not nominate themselves.

Anyone interested in becoming a World Fellow should instead sign up to receive notification when the official application becomes available.

2. Nomination Is Not Required

Candidates do not need to be nominated to apply for the World Fellows Program.

The nomination process is simply an outreach effort designed to help Yale identify exceptional leaders.

3. Nominations Do Not Influence Selection

Yale clearly states that:

  • Nominated candidates receive no preference during the application review process.
  • Nomination does not increase an applicant’s likelihood of selection.
  • Every application is evaluated equally once applications officially open.

4. Multiple Nominations Do Not Improve Chances

Receiving multiple nominations does not increase a candidate’s chances of being selected.

The programme evaluates candidates based on their own merits rather than the number of recommendations received.

5. Multiple Candidates May Be Nominated

Individuals are welcome to nominate more than one outstanding leader.

Each nomination should be submitted separately.

Information Required About the Nominee

The nomination form requests several pieces of information regarding the individual being nominated.

Required information includes:

  • First name
  • Last name or surname
  • Email address

Nominators must also answer the following questions:

Why would this person make a great Yale World Fellow?

This section allows nominators to explain:

  • Leadership achievements
  • Professional accomplishments
  • Innovation
  • Community impact
  • Vision
  • Potential for future leadership

Relationship with the Nominee

Nominators are also asked to describe:

  • How long they have known the nominee.
  • The capacity in which they know the nominee.

This provides important context for the recommendation.

Information Required About the Nominator

Individuals submitting nominations will also provide their own information.

Required details include:

  • First name
  • Last name or surname
  • Email address

An optional field allows nominators to include:

  • Professional title
  • Organization

What Happens After a Nomination Is Submitted?

After a nomination is successfully submitted, Yale University sends an email directly to the nominated individual.

The email informs the nominee that someone has encouraged them to apply to the Yale Maurice R. Greenberg World Fellows Program.

The message also includes:

  • Additional information about the programme.
  • Guidance regarding the future application process.

This ensures that nominated individuals are aware of the opportunity when applications officially open.

Current Application Status

At present, the World Fellows Program is not accepting applications.

Individuals interested in becoming World Fellows are encouraged to sign up for notifications so they can be informed when the official application process begins.

Meanwhile, members of the public may continue submitting nominations for suitable candidates.

Why Nominate Someone?

Nominating an outstanding leader helps connect talented professionals with one of Yale University’s premier international leadership programmes.

By submitting a nomination, you can help ensure that deserving changemakers receive information about an opportunity that may significantly contribute to their leadership development and global impact.

The programme aims to cultivate a community of leaders committed to addressing today’s most pressing international challenges through innovation, collaboration, and thoughtful leadership.

How to Submit a Nomination

Individuals interested in nominating a candidate should:

  1. Identify an outstanding emerging leader.
  2. Confirm that the individual is not submitting a self-nomination.
  3. Complete the official nomination form.
  4. Provide accurate information about the nominee.
  5. Explain why the nominee would make an excellent Yale World Fellow.
  6. Describe your relationship with the nominee.
  7. Submit the completed nomination form.

Following submission, Yale University will notify the nominated individual and provide additional information about the programme.

Click HERE to learn more and to nominate an outstanding Global Leader for the Yale Maurice R. Greenberg World Fellows Program 2027.

Conclusion

The Yale Maurice R. Greenberg World Fellows Program 2027 continues its commitment to identifying exceptional emerging leaders who are creating innovative solutions to global challenges. Through its open nomination process, Yale University invites people from around the world to recommend individuals whose leadership, creativity, and impact deserve international recognition.

Although applications for the 2027 cohort are not yet open, the nomination process offers an excellent opportunity to help outstanding professionals learn about one of Yale University’s most respected leadership programmes. Whether working in public service, business, civil society, technology, education, research, or another field, rising leaders with demonstrated impact and strong potential are encouraged to be nominated so they can consider applying when the official application cycle begins.

Discover more global Fellowship and Leadership Development opportunities on OFY.

The post Yale Maurice R. Greenberg World Fellows Program 2027: Nominate Outstanding Global Leaders for One of Yale University’s Prestigious Leadership Programmes appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Call for Applications: Feminist Africa Online Writing Workshop 2026
item.dc:creator: Nana Safo
item.description:
Are you an Africa-based early career researcher working on feminist or gender analysis in the social sciences? The Feminist Africa Journal is inviting eligible researchers to apply for its 4-day Online Writing Workshop, scheduled to take place from 16 to 19 November 2026. The workshop is designed to support early career scholars in transforming draft […]

The post Call for Applications: Feminist Africa Online Writing Workshop 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you an Africa-based early career researcher working on feminist or gender analysis in the social sciences? The Feminist Africa Journal is inviting eligible researchers to apply for its 4-day Online Writing Workshop, scheduled to take place from 16 to 19 November 2026.

The workshop is designed to support early career scholars in transforming draft research papers into publishable journal articles while strengthening their academic writing, feminist research, and publication skills.

About Feminist Africa

Feminist Africa is a continental Gender Studies Journal established in 2002 at the African Gender Institute of the University of Cape Town. Following the Yiri Consensus of 2018, the journal moved to the Institute of African Studies at the University of Ghana.

For more than two decades, Feminist Africa has provided a platform for intellectual and activist research, dialogue, and strategy focused on postcolonial feminism.

The journal describes itself as a transformative, anti-imperialist, and anti-colonial scholarly journal committed to “building skills as it grows” through collaborative training interventions, internships, and workshops.

About the Online Writing Workshop

The 4-day online workshop is designed to provide Africa-based early career researchers with the intellectual space, mentorship, and training needed to develop a draft research paper into a publishable journal article.

The programme responds to the challenge that much of the feminist research conducted, particularly at the graduate level, remains unpublished and therefore has limited visibility and impact beyond academic theses.

Through seminars, writing sessions, and mentorship, the workshop will help participants strengthen their research-to-publication journey and deepen the impact of feminist scholarship produced by early career researchers in Africa.

Objectives of the Workshop

The workshop aims to:

  • Strengthen the academic writing skills of African early career scholars, with particular attention to female scholars.
  • Improve the success rate of early career African scholars submitting manuscripts to high-quality journals.
  • Build a network of Africa-based scholars working on feminist and gender analysis.

Workshop Structure

The 4-day programme will be organised as an online writing retreat featuring daily seminars and dedicated writing sessions.

The seminars will include:

  • Practical guidance on academic writing.
  • Mentorship on formulating feminist research questions.
  • Guidance on publishing in high-quality academic journals.
  • Support for developing draft research into a publishable journal article.

Funding Support

Successful applicants will receive funding support to help cover the cost of high-speed internet access during the workshop.

Eligibility

Applications are open to early career researchers who meet the following criteria:

  • Hold a doctorate awarded within five years of the application deadline, meaning the doctoral certificate must have been awarded after July 2021 and by July 2026.
  • Are employed at a recognised university or research institute in Sub-Saharan Africa.
  • Are fluent in written and spoken English, as no translation services will be provided.

The selection criteria also indicate that applicants may be considered if they have written evidence from a supervisor confirming teaching and satisfactory research progress towards a PhD over the last five years.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

  1. A motivation letter of no more than one page explaining their interest in the workshop and what they hope to gain from participating.
  2. A draft basic research paper of 5,000–8,000 words in English containing original data and preliminary data analysis.
  3. An abstract of the research paper of no more than 500 words.
  4. A full CV.
  5. A PhD award certificate, where applicable.

Selection Criteria

Applications will be assessed through a strictly merit-based selection process.

The main selection criteria include:

  • A PhD certificate granted between July 2021 and July 2026, or written evidence from a supervisor of teaching and satisfactory research progress towards a PhD over the last five years.
  • Employment at a university or research institute on the African continent.
  • Excellence, relevance, and originality of the draft research paper.
  • Strength of the paper’s gender or feminist analysis.

Workshop Dates

📅 16–19 November 2026

Application Deadline

⏰ Monday, 3 August 2026

How to Apply

Applications should be submitted by email to: 📧 conference@feministafrica.net

For more information on this workshop, visit Feminist Africa HERE

Also, for more workshop opportunities, visit OFY Website HERE

This is a valuable opportunity for early career African researchers working in feminist and gender studies to receive writing support, mentorship, and guidance towards publishing their research in high-quality academic journals.

If you meet the eligibility requirements, prepare your documents early and submit your application before the deadline.

The post Call for Applications: Feminist Africa Online Writing Workshop 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: RMIT Vice-Chancellor’s Research Fellowships 2026: Apply for Prestigious Research Positions in Australia
item.dc:creator: Ning Ma
item.description:
Applications are now open for the RMIT Vice-Chancellor’s Research Fellowships 2026, a prestigious programme designed to attract outstanding local and international researchers who want to conduct applied, transdisciplinary research with measurable benefits for society, the economy and the environment. Each year, RMIT University appoints talented researchers and research teams to work on complex real-world challenges. […]

The post RMIT Vice-Chancellor’s Research Fellowships 2026: Apply for Prestigious Research Positions in Australia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the RMIT Vice-Chancellor’s Research Fellowships 2026, a prestigious programme designed to attract outstanding local and international researchers who want to conduct applied, transdisciplinary research with measurable benefits for society, the economy and the environment.

Each year, RMIT University appoints talented researchers and research teams to work on complex real-world challenges. The fellowship programme provides successful applicants with the institutional support, professional development opportunities and research environment needed to build influential academic careers and translate research into practical impact.

For the 2026 recruitment round, RMIT is seeking applicants whose work aligns with three strategic research areas:

  • Regenerative and Sustainable Futures
  • MedTech Innovation
  • Digital Innovation

The fellowships are available across four categories covering academic levels A to D. Applicants must hold a PhD or equivalent doctoral qualification and satisfy the specific eligibility requirements for their chosen fellowship category.

Applications close at 11:59 a.m. AEST on Wednesday, 29 July 2026.

About the RMIT Vice-Chancellor’s Research Fellowships Program

The RMIT Vice-Chancellor’s Research Fellowships Program is part of the university’s long-term strategy to strengthen research and innovation with real-world impact.

The programme seeks researchers who can work across traditional academic boundaries and collaborate with industry, government, communities and other research partners. Fellows are expected to contribute not only to academic knowledge but also to solutions that address major social, environmental, technological and economic challenges.

RMIT is particularly interested in researchers who demonstrate:

  • Excellence within their academic or professional field
  • Creativity and imagination in research design
  • Strong potential for interdisciplinary collaboration
  • A commitment to producing impact beyond academia
  • Alignment with RMIT’s strategic research priorities
  • An ability to build connections across disciplines, organisations and communities

Appointments will be made on merit, with particular attention given to the applicant’s alignment with the nominated research focus area and its associated high-priority topics.

Research Priority Areas for the 2026 Recruitment Round

The 2026 fellowships focus on three broad areas intended to support RMIT’s research and innovation ambitions.

Regenerative and Sustainable Futures

This research area focuses on advancing regenerative and sustainable approaches that restore, renew and revitalise environmental, economic and social systems.

Research may contribute to the development of new ways of responding to challenges such as environmental degradation, climate change, resource pressures, social inequality and unsustainable production or consumption.

Applicants working in this area should demonstrate how their research could contribute to more resilient and sustainable futures.

Relevant approaches may involve collaboration across fields such as:

  • Environmental science
  • Sustainability
  • Engineering
  • Architecture and urban development
  • Social sciences
  • Economics
  • Design
  • Policy
  • Climate research
  • Circular and regenerative systems

Applicants should consult the official fellowship information for the specific high-priority topics accepted under this research area.

MedTech Innovation

The MedTech Innovation priority seeks research that can transform health outcomes through advances in medical technologies.

The focus is on innovation that improves the prevention, diagnosis, monitoring or treatment of health conditions and strengthens the wider healthcare system.

Potential research may involve areas such as:

  • Medical devices
  • Health technologies
  • Biomedical engineering
  • Diagnostics
  • Digital health
  • Therapeutic innovation
  • Health data
  • Patient-centred technology
  • Translational medical research

Applicants should explain how their research can move beyond theory and contribute to improved health outcomes, healthcare delivery or medical practice.

Digital Innovation

The Digital Innovation priority supports multidisciplinary research that contributes to a prosperous, secure and responsible digital future.

RMIT is seeking research that responds to the opportunities and risks created by rapid technological development.

Relevant work may relate to:

  • Artificial intelligence
  • Cybersecurity
  • Digital systems
  • Data science
  • Emerging technologies
  • Digital economies
  • Human-computer interaction
  • Responsible technology
  • Digital inclusion
  • Technology governance

Successful proposals should demonstrate strong research quality while addressing the broader social, economic, ethical or security implications of digital transformation.

Fellowship Categories and Academic Levels

The RMIT Vice-Chancellor’s Research Fellowships are available through four categories covering academic levels A to D.

These levels represent different stages of an academic research career. The programme may therefore include opportunities for researchers ranging from early-career scholars to highly experienced research leaders.

The exact eligibility rules, expected achievements and research experience requirements differ by fellowship category.

Applicants should carefully review the relevant category before applying, particularly in relation to:

  • Years since completion of the PhD
  • Academic level
  • Research track record
  • Publication record
  • Leadership experience
  • Industry or community engagement
  • Evidence of research impact
  • Ability to attract research funding
  • Career interruptions or special circumstances

Applicants should not assume that holding a PhD alone guarantees eligibility for every category.

Benefits of the RMIT Vice-Chancellor’s Research Fellowships

Successful fellows will receive access to a supportive research environment designed to help them build strong and sustainable careers.

The programme offers:

Prestigious Research Fellowship Appointments

Fellowships are available across academic levels A to D, allowing researchers at different career stages to compete within an appropriate category.

Research Support Funding

Successful fellows may receive generous research support funding.

The amount and conditions depend on the selected fellowship category. Applicants should consult the category-specific information for exact details.

Access to RMIT Research Facilities

Fellows will be able to use RMIT’s research facilities, institutional resources and specialist infrastructure relevant to their work.

Interdisciplinary Networks

RMIT has more than 40 interdisciplinary networks developed through its eight Enabling Impact Platforms.

These networks create opportunities for fellows to:

  • Collaborate across academic disciplines
  • Connect with researchers working on related challenges
  • Build industry and community partnerships
  • Develop joint research projects
  • Increase the reach and relevance of their research

Mentoring

Fellows will receive mentoring to support their research development, career planning and integration into the RMIT research environment.

Researcher Development Programmes

Successful applicants will have access to professional development programmes designed to help researchers identify new pathways to impact.

These programmes may support skills related to research leadership, collaboration, communication, knowledge translation and engagement beyond academia.

Flexible and Supportive Workplace

RMIT describes itself as a recognised employer of choice with a progressive, inclusive and values-based culture.

The university emphasises flexibility, ethical research, diversity and a supportive working environment.

Why Conduct Research at RMIT?

RMIT University places strong emphasis on research that produces meaningful outcomes outside the university.

The institution’s research environment is characterised by:

  • A focus on excellence and impact
  • Strong industry and community engagement
  • A commitment to translating research into practical outcomes
  • Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration
  • A global outlook
  • Civic responsibility
  • Support for diverse research topics and impact pathways
  • A progressive and inclusive institutional culture

RMIT’s approach may be particularly attractive to researchers who want to collaborate with partners outside academia and apply their expertise to practical challenges.

Who Can Apply?

The fellowships are open to both:

  • Australian researchers
  • International researchers

Applicants may apply from any country, provided they meet the required academic and category-specific criteria.

The programme is intended for outstanding researchers whose work aligns with the 2026 research priorities and who can demonstrate the potential to make a positive real-world contribution.

General Eligibility Requirements

All applicants must:

  1. Hold a PhD, doctorate or equivalent doctoral qualification.
  2. Complete and submit the application form before the 2026 deadline.
  3. Meet the additional eligibility criteria for the selected fellowship category.
  4. Demonstrate strong alignment with one of the three strategic research areas.
  5. Show evidence of research excellence and potential impact.

RMIT employees who currently hold ongoing academic appointments are not eligible for the Vice-Chancellor’s Research Fellowships.

Applicants holding other forms of employment or association with RMIT should review the official eligibility rules to confirm whether they may apply.

Selection Considerations

Appointments will be made on merit.

Applications are expected to be assessed according to factors such as:

  • Research quality
  • Academic achievements
  • Alignment with RMIT’s strategic research priorities
  • Potential for societal, environmental or economic impact
  • Creativity and originality
  • Interdisciplinary potential
  • Career stage and fellowship category
  • Ability to collaborate with external partners
  • Evidence of research translation
  • Potential contribution to RMIT’s research environment

Applicants should clearly demonstrate how their proposed research fits the selected priority area rather than relying on a broad or indirect connection.

How to Prepare a Strong Application

A competitive application should clearly explain:

1. The Research Problem

Identify the specific challenge or research gap the proposed work will address.

2. Alignment With the Priority Area

Explain how the proposal fits Regenerative and Sustainable Futures, MedTech Innovation or Digital Innovation.

3. Research Approach

Describe the methods, theoretical framework and interdisciplinary elements of the proposed work.

4. Expected Impact

Show how the research could create benefits beyond academic publication.

These benefits may relate to:

  • Society
  • Public policy
  • Communities
  • Industry
  • Healthcare
  • Technology
  • The environment
  • Economic development

5. Applicant’s Track Record

Provide evidence of relevant research achievements, publications, collaborations, leadership and engagement.

6. Contribution to RMIT

Explain how the applicant will contribute to RMIT’s research culture, interdisciplinary networks and strategic goals.

7. Career Development

Describe how the fellowship will support the applicant’s development and future research leadership.

Applicants should also ensure that their materials address the requirements of the correct academic category.

Application Process

Applications for the 2026 round must be submitted through the official RMIT application process.

Before applying, candidates should:

  • Review the four fellowship categories
  • Confirm their eligibility
  • Select the most appropriate research priority
  • Read the high-priority topics under that research area
  • Prepare all required documents
  • Complete every section of the application form
  • Submit before the stated deadline

Applicants should rely on the official application page for the complete list of required documents and any instructions concerning references, research proposals, CVs or supporting materials.

APPLY HERE

Application Deadline

Applications close at:

11:59 a.m. Australian Eastern Standard Time on Wednesday, 29 July 2026

International applicants should convert the deadline into their local time zone and submit early. The closing time is in the morning under AEST, not at the end of the calendar day.

Late or incomplete applications may not be considered.

Conclusion

The RMIT Vice-Chancellor’s Research Fellowships 2026 offer an important opportunity for talented researchers seeking to build influential careers while addressing major global challenges.

Through generous research support, access to facilities, mentoring, interdisciplinary networks and professional development, the programme provides an environment in which fellows can pursue ambitious applied research.

Researchers working in Regenerative and Sustainable Futures, MedTech Innovation or Digital Innovation are encouraged to review the category-specific requirements and prepare applications that clearly demonstrate excellence, alignment and real-world impact.

Applications must be submitted by 11:59 a.m. AEST on Wednesday, 29 July 2026.

For further information and application requirements, CLICK HERE.

To discover more fellowship opportunities, VISIT HERE.

The post RMIT Vice-Chancellor’s Research Fellowships 2026: Apply for Prestigious Research Positions in Australia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Canvas Home Small Grants 2026 for Artisan Groups in Developing Countries | Up to $1,500 in Funding for Craft Businesses and Cooperatives
item.dc:creator: Bob Waudo
item.description:
Artisan groups and craft-producing organizations in developing countries now have an exciting opportunity to access financial support through the Canvas Home Small Grants 2026 program. Administered by HANDMADE to MARKET (HtoM), a division of Creative Learning, this initiative is designed to strengthen artisan enterprises by providing funding for materials, equipment, training, marketing, and other business […]

The post Canvas Home Small Grants 2026 for Artisan Groups in Developing Countries | Up to $1,500 in Funding for Craft Businesses and Cooperatives appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Artisan groups and craft-producing organizations in developing countries now have an exciting opportunity to access financial support through the Canvas Home Small Grants 2026 program. Administered by HANDMADE to MARKET (HtoM), a division of Creative Learning, this initiative is designed to strengthen artisan enterprises by providing funding for materials, equipment, training, marketing, and other business development activities.

The program offers grants of up to $1,500 to eligible artisan cooperatives, community-based organizations, and small craft businesses seeking to expand employment opportunities, improve production, preserve traditional crafts, and generate sustainable income.

If your artisan organization is looking for financial assistance to scale production, improve product quality, access new markets, or strengthen business operations, this grant could provide the support needed to grow sustainably.

Application Deadline: August 17, 2026, at 11:59 PM Eastern Standard Time (EST).

About HANDMADE to MARKET (HtoM)

HANDMADE to MARKET (HtoM) is a division of Creative Learning, a Washington, D.C.-based 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to empowering artisan businesses around the world.

The organization works with artisan communities by providing:

  • Access to international markets
  • Business development training
  • Eco-effective production methods
  • Design innovation
  • Marketing support
  • Strategic partnerships
  • Community-building initiatives

Through these efforts, HtoM seeks to promote sustainable economic growth while preserving cultural heritage and improving the livelihoods of artisan communities worldwide.

Canvas Home Small Grants 2026 Overview

The Canvas Home Small Grants program exists to strengthen established artisan groups by helping them obtain the resources necessary to improve production, increase sales, and create more employment opportunities.

Each successful applicant may receive funding of up to $1,500.

Rather than supporting individual artisans or new businesses, the program focuses on strengthening existing craft-producing organizations that already have an established structure and are ready to grow.

The grants help artisan groups become more competitive while preserving traditional craftsmanship and promoting environmentally responsible production practices.

Grant Value

Successful applicants may receive grants of up to $1,500.

Funding is intended to help artisan organizations:

  • Expand employment opportunities
  • Increase production capacity
  • Improve product quality
  • Reach new markets
  • Increase income generation
  • Strengthen long-term sustainability

Types of Grants Available

Applicants may apply for only one of the following grant categories.

1. Materials, Tools, Equipment, and Instruction Grant

This funding category supports improvements in craft production through the purchase of essential resources and technical support.

Eligible activities include:

  • Purchasing raw materials
  • Buying specialized craft tools
  • Purchasing production equipment
  • Establishing raw material banks
  • Creating shared tool banks
  • Setting up centralized artisan workshops
  • Funding technical assistance
  • Hiring trainers for capacity-building workshops
  • Product design training
  • Marketing training
  • Business management training
  • Technical skills development
  • Purchasing digital cameras
  • Internet connectivity costs
  • Website development

The goal is to improve production efficiency while enhancing artisans’ technical and business capabilities.

2. Marketing Support Grant

This category is designed for artisan groups that already have established production systems and existing sales.

The grant helps organizations expand into new markets and attract additional customers.

Funding may be used for:

  • Craft fair participation
  • Exhibition stand costs
  • Display shelving
  • Marketing brochures
  • Product catalogs
  • Promotional materials
  • Market expansion initiatives
  • Launching new products
  • Entering new domestic or international markets

This category is ideal for organizations ready to grow beyond their current customer base.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify, applicants must satisfy all eligibility requirements.

Eligible Applicants

The program primarily supports established craft-producing organizations such as:

  • Artisan cooperatives
  • Community-based organizations
  • Small artisan businesses
  • Craft-producing groups

The primary focus is on organizations located in developing countries.

Eligible Craft Products

Supported products must:

  • Be handmade
  • Have artistic value
  • Serve a functional purpose
  • Reflect cultural significance

Applicants must submit photographs demonstrating the products.

Recommendation Letter Requirement

Every application must include a:

  • Signed recommendation letter
  • Dated recommendation letter

The recommendation should come from an organization familiar with the applicant’s work.

Suitable organizations include:

  • Previous funding organizations
  • Development partners
  • National craft organizations
  • International nonprofit organizations
  • Organizations with direct knowledge of the artisan group

The recommendation must be credible and independently written.

Applications without a signed and dated recommendation letter will automatically be rejected.

Important notes include:

  • Letters from previous Canvas Home Small Grant recipients are not accepted.
  • Recommendation letters must not be purchased.
  • Recommenders must not charge fees.

Language Requirements

Applications must:

  • Be written entirely in English
  • Use only U.S. Dollar (USD) currency

Photo Requirements

Applicants must submit:

  • At least five recent photographs
  • Photos taken within the previous twelve months

The photographs should clearly showcase the artisan products.

Follow-Up Reporting Requirement

Grant recipients agree to submit a follow-up report approximately six months after receiving the grant.

This report helps HtoM evaluate:

  • Grant impact
  • Business growth
  • Project implementation
  • Outcomes achieved

Banking Requirements

Applicants must have access to a bank account capable of receiving:

  • International wire transfers
  • U.S. Dollar (USD) payments

Recipients are responsible for:

  • Currency conversion fees
  • Bank transaction charges
  • Any costs associated with receiving grant funds

Selection Priorities

Because funding is limited, HtoM gives preference to applications that:

  • Provide detailed project explanations
  • Include strong justifications for funding
  • Benefit large numbers of artisans
  • Promote cultural preservation
  • Demonstrate environmental sustainability
  • Clearly explain financial need
  • Present a realistic implementation plan
  • Identify specific markets for finished products
  • Have reliable communication contacts
  • Can provide regular project updates
  • Have never previously received a Canvas Home Small Grant

Although previous recipients may apply, first-time applicants receive preference.

Activities Not Supported

Canvas Home Small Grants will not fund:

  • Individual artisans
  • New craft start-up projects
  • Building trade projects
  • Industrial or household products lacking handmade artistic value
  • Salaries
  • Staff wages
  • Travel expenses
  • Vehicle purchases
  • Environmentally harmful production practices
  • Projects using uncertified timber from sensitive ecosystems
  • Independent grant writers working without close relationships with artisan groups
  • Designers or exporters who do not directly work with artisans

Applicants should carefully review these exclusions before applying.

Application Timeline

The application process follows the schedule below.

Activity Date
Applications Open July 15, 2026
Application Deadline August 17, 2026 (11:59 PM EST)
Internal Review August 2026
Award Notifications September 2026
Grant Payments First week of October 2026

Late or incomplete applications will not be reviewed.

Application Process

Applicants should complete every section of the online application carefully.

The review process includes two stages.

Stage One

HtoM staff verify:

  • Eligibility
  • Required documents
  • Completeness of the application

Staff do not evaluate project quality during this stage.

Stage Two

Eligible applications are forwarded to an independent review committee responsible for selecting grant recipients.

Applicants will receive notification by email whether they are selected or not.

Successful applicants will receive funding after confirming their banking information.

Required Documents

Applicants should prepare:

  • Completed online application
  • Signed recommendation letter
  • Five recent product photographs
  • Supporting documentation requested within the application

Preparing these materials before beginning the application can make the submission process easier.

How to Apply

Interested organizations should submit their applications through the official Canvas Home Small Grants Application Form.

Applicants are encouraged to:

  1. Preview all application questions before starting.
  2. Review the Canvas Home Small Grants Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ).
  3. Prepare all required supporting documents.
  4. Create a free Jotform account to save progress and return later if necessary.
  5. Submit the completed application before the deadline.

Applicants unable to complete the online application due to technical issues may request a PDF application by contacting:

RachelL@creativelearning.org

However, PDF submissions are accepted only with prior approval from HANDMADE to MARKET.

For technical support during the application process, applicants should also contact the same email address.

APPLY HERE

Why You Should Apply

The Canvas Home Small Grants Program provides much more than financial assistance.

It enables artisan organizations to:

  • Increase production capacity
  • Preserve traditional craftsmanship
  • Improve product quality
  • Strengthen business operations
  • Reach international markets
  • Build sustainable livelihoods
  • Create employment opportunities
  • Promote environmentally responsible production
  • Expand income-generating opportunities
  • Strengthen community development

For artisan organizations seeking practical funding to support growth while preserving cultural heritage, this grant represents an excellent opportunity.

Visit HERE to learn more about the Canvas Home Small Grants 2026 for Artisan Groups in Developing Countries

Important Deadline

Application Deadline: August 17, 2026, at 11:59 PM Eastern Standard Time (EST).

Applications submitted after the deadline or missing required documentation will not be considered.

Conclusion

The Canvas Home Small Grants 2026 program offers a valuable opportunity for established artisan organizations in developing countries to strengthen their businesses through targeted financial support. With grants of up to $1,500, eligible groups can invest in better materials, equipment, technical training, marketing initiatives, and business development activities that improve productivity and expand market opportunities.

Beyond financial assistance, the program emphasizes cultural preservation, environmental sustainability, and long-term community development, making it an important initiative for artisan groups committed to creating lasting economic impact. Interested organizations should begin preparing their applications, recommendation letters, product photographs, and supporting documents early to ensure they submit a complete application before the August 17, 2026 deadline.

For more global Grant opportunities visit OFY HERE

The post Canvas Home Small Grants 2026 for Artisan Groups in Developing Countries | Up to $1,500 in Funding for Craft Businesses and Cooperatives appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: European Commission OCT Youth Network 2026–2027: Fully Funded One-Year Leadership Opportunity for Young People from Overseas Countries and Territories
item.dc:creator: Fridah Kamuri
item.description:
The European Commission has officially opened applications for the 2026–2027 Overseas Countries and Territories (OCT) Youth Network, offering an exceptional one-year leadership and learning opportunity for young people from the 13 Overseas Countries and Territories associated with the European Union. Designed to strengthen the partnership between the European Union and the Overseas Countries and Territories, […]

The post European Commission OCT Youth Network 2026–2027: Fully Funded One-Year Leadership Opportunity for Young People from Overseas Countries and Territories appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The European Commission has officially opened applications for the 2026–2027 Overseas Countries and Territories (OCT) Youth Network, offering an exceptional one-year leadership and learning opportunity for young people from the 13 Overseas Countries and Territories associated with the European Union.

Designed to strengthen the partnership between the European Union and the Overseas Countries and Territories, the OCT Youth Network equips young leaders with knowledge about European institutions, policymaking, sustainable development, and international cooperation while creating lasting connections among youth from different territories.

Participants will embark on an engaging one-year journey that combines study visits, leadership dialogues, networking events, online learning sessions, and participation in international forums. The programme empowers young people to represent the voices of their communities while contributing fresh ideas to the future of the OCT-EU Partnership.

If you are passionate about leadership, international affairs, youth participation, diplomacy, public policy, sustainable development, or European cooperation, this programme offers an invaluable opportunity to broaden your horizons and become part of an influential international youth community.

What is the OCT Youth Network?

The Overseas Countries and Territories Youth Network is a prestigious initiative established by the European Commission to strengthen engagement between young people living in Overseas Countries and Territories and the European Union.

The Network consists of 25 carefully selected young people between the ages of 20 and 28, representing all thirteen Overseas Countries and Territories associated with the EU.

Since its launch in 2022, the programme has become one of the flagship youth engagement initiatives supporting stronger collaboration between the European Union and its Overseas Countries and Territories.

Rather than being a traditional training programme, the Network serves as a platform where young people actively contribute ideas, exchange experiences, influence dialogue, and represent youth perspectives within the OCT-EU Partnership.

Members spend an entire year participating in collaborative learning activities while building professional relationships with policymakers, EU institutions, fellow youth leaders, and experts from across the territories.

Purpose of the OCT Youth Network

The initiative was created to increase youth participation in international cooperation and ensure that young voices play an important role in shaping the future of the OCT-EU Partnership.

Its primary objectives include:

  • Connecting young people from all 13 Overseas Countries and Territories.
  • Building stronger relationships between OCT youth and European Union institutions.
  • Increasing awareness and understanding of the European Union across the OCTs.
  • Giving young people opportunities to contribute directly to important policy discussions.
  • Promoting dialogue between youth and political decision-makers.
  • Creating stronger links between OCT youth initiatives and other regional, national, and European youth programmes.
  • Encouraging intercultural understanding among young people from diverse backgrounds.
  • Supporting meaningful youth participation in sustainable development.

What Participants Will Experience

Successful applicants become members of a dynamic international network and participate in numerous educational and leadership activities throughout the year.

The programme includes:

1. Study Visit to Brussels

One of the highlights of the programme is a week-long study visit to Brussels, Belgium.

During this visit, members will:

  • Explore how European institutions function.
  • Visit major EU institutions.
  • Learn how European policies are developed.
  • Meet European policymakers.
  • Participate in interactive workshops.
  • Engage in structured dialogue sessions.
  • Exchange ideas with European leaders.
  • Learn more about the OCT-EU Partnership.

The Brussels experience gives participants firsthand exposure to European governance and international cooperation.

2. Online Learning Sessions

Throughout the year, members participate in various virtual learning activities including:

  • Interactive webinars.
  • Capacity-building workshops.
  • Educational seminars.
  • Skills development sessions.
  • Policy discussions.
  • Expert presentations.

These sessions deepen participants’ understanding of:

  • European institutions
  • International partnerships
  • Sustainable development
  • Youth participation
  • Leadership
  • Governance
  • Public policy

3. Networking Opportunities

Participants become part of a growing international youth community.

Networking opportunities include:

  • Connecting with youth leaders from all 13 OCTs.
  • Building relationships with EU institutions.
  • Meeting government representatives.
  • Engaging with international organisations.
  • Connecting with policymakers.
  • Collaborating with experts and stakeholders.
  • Building lifelong friendships and professional relationships.

4. Participation in International Events

Network members participate in several prestigious international events.

These may include:

  • OCT-EU Forum
  • Climate-related conferences
  • European Youth events
  • International policy dialogues
  • High-level stakeholder meetings

Participation allows young leaders to share perspectives from their territories while contributing to important international discussions.

5. Youth Representation

Members play an active role in ensuring young people’s voices are heard.

They contribute by:

  • Presenting youth recommendations.
  • Participating in dialogue sessions.
  • Co-leading discussions.
  • Helping identify priorities for the Network.
  • Sharing experiences from their territories.
  • Promoting youth perspectives during high-level events.

Benefits of Joining the OCT Youth Network

Participants gain numerous academic, professional, and personal benefits throughout the programme.

These include:

  • One-year international leadership experience.
  • Participation in a study visit to Brussels.
  • Direct exposure to European institutions.
  • Access to EU policymakers and decision-makers.
  • Expanded international professional network.
  • Opportunities to represent youth interests.
  • Participation in international conferences and forums.
  • Leadership development.
  • Improved communication skills.
  • Greater understanding of international cooperation.
  • Cross-cultural learning experiences.
  • Collaboration with young leaders from different territories.
  • Enhanced knowledge of European governance.
  • Personal growth and increased global awareness.

Diversity and Inclusion

The European Commission emphasizes diversity within the Network.

The OCT Youth Network welcomes applicants from a broad range of backgrounds, including individuals with different:

  • Educational qualifications.
  • Professional experiences.
  • Areas of expertise.
  • Social backgrounds.
  • Cultural identities.
  • Personal interests.
  • Economic circumstances.

This diversity strengthens dialogue and promotes inclusive decision-making.

History of the OCT Youth Network

First Edition (2022–2023)

The inaugural Network brought together 25 young people representing 10 Overseas Countries and Territories.

One of its major achievements was the development of a Youth Declaration, which was presented during the OCT-EU Forum in Nouméa, New Caledonia in November 2022.

The declaration highlighted young people’s priorities regarding the OCT-EU Partnership.

Second Edition (2023–2024)

The second cohort included representatives from all 13 OCTs.

Members participated in:

  • Study visits to Brussels.
  • Networking activities.
  • Policy discussions.
  • Cultural exchanges.

A major achievement was the presentation of:

  • A Youth Declaration.
  • A photo exhibition.
  • A video exhibition.

These showcased the importance of cultural diversity and stronger exchanges between the OCTs and the European Union during the OCT-EU Forum held in Brussels in February 2024.

Third Edition (2024–2025)

The third cohort continued expanding the Network’s impact.

Highlights included:

  • Structured dialogue with Younous Omarjee, Vice-President of the European Parliament responsible for OCT matters.
  • Participation in the Climate Finance Forum in Brussels.
  • Attendance at the European Youth Event in Strasbourg.
  • Representation at the United Nations Ocean Conference in Nice.

These experiences demonstrated the growing influence of OCT youth within international discussions.

Why the European Union Created the OCT Youth Network

The initiative reflects the European Union’s increasing commitment to youth participation in global development.

It aligns closely with the Youth Action Plan in EU External Action, adopted in October 2022.

The Action Plan recognizes young people as key partners in building:

  • Democratic societies.
  • Inclusive communities.
  • Peaceful societies.
  • Sustainable development.
  • Strong international partnerships.

The OCT Youth Network is one practical way the European Union is turning this commitment into action.

How to Apply

Applications are submitted through the official European Commission application process.

Interested candidates should carefully review the eligibility requirements and complete the application before the deadline.

Applicants are encouraged to prepare thoughtful responses that demonstrate:

  • Leadership potential.
  • Motivation.
  • Interest in international cooperation.
  • Commitment to representing young people from their territory.
  • Enthusiasm for contributing to the OCT-EU Partnership.

APPLY HERE

Application Deadline

30 August 2026 (23:59 CEST)

Late applications are not expected to be considered.

Why You Should Apply

The OCT Youth Network is much more than a youth programme—it is a gateway to international leadership, diplomacy, policymaking, and global collaboration.

Participants gain unique access to European institutions, engage directly with policymakers, represent young people from their territories, and build lasting international friendships while contributing meaningfully to the future of the OCT-EU Partnership.

For young people seeking to develop leadership skills, expand their international network, and play an active role in shaping youth engagement between the European Union and the Overseas Countries and Territories, this programme offers an exceptional platform for personal and professional growth.

Visit HERE to learn more about the European Commission OCT Youth Network 2026–2027

Conclusion

The European Commission’s 2026–2027 Overseas Countries and Territories Youth Network presents an outstanding opportunity for ambitious young leaders from the 13 Overseas Countries and Territories associated with the European Union. Through a comprehensive one-year programme featuring study visits, online learning, policy engagement, international networking, and participation in prestigious global events, selected participants will gain invaluable knowledge, leadership experience, and lifelong professional connections. If you meet the eligibility requirements and are passionate about representing your community while contributing to the future of the OCT-EU Partnership, this is an opportunity not to miss.

Application Deadline: 30 August 2026 (23:59 CEST)

For more global Fellowship opportunities, visit OFY HERE 

The post European Commission OCT Youth Network 2026–2027: Fully Funded One-Year Leadership Opportunity for Young People from Overseas Countries and Territories appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Leo’s Experimental Micrograntting Program 2026 | $10,000 Grants for Bold Ideas to Help Humanity Navigate AGI
item.dc:creator: Evelyn Essuman
item.description:
